title: The Seven Saints of Marrakech
description: Seven Sufi mystics, seven tombs, one pilgrimage route. Walk the path that most tourists never know exists.
bonus_card: The pilgrimage was formalised by Moulay Ismail to rival Fes. It's still walked today — mostly by Moroccans, rarely by outsiders. By completing this trail, you've done something most visitors to Marrakech never will.

title: The Guild Secrets
description: Tanners, dyers, metalworkers, zellige masters. The craft guilds of the medina have guarded their techniques for centuries.
bonus_card: Marrakech's craft guilds operate like medieval European guilds — knowledge passed from master to apprentice, family dynasties controlling specific trades. The techniques you've seen today have not been published in any book. They were shown to you by proximity.

title: The Women of Marrakech
description: The women whose names were never in the guidebooks. The power behind the thrones, the midwives who crossed religious lines, the builders who were written out of history.
bonus_card: Moroccan women have shaped this city for a thousand years. The medina was built by men but sustained by women — the ones who ran the households, managed the trades, raised the children who became the sultans. Their names are mostly lost. But their work is every wall you've touched today.

title: The Invisible River
description: Beneath the streets of Marrakech flows an ancient water system. Built a thousand years ago. Still functioning. Completely invisible.
bonus_card: Marrakech was founded because of water — the khettaras brought snowmelt from the Atlas mountains through underground channels. Without them, the city could not exist. When you drink tap water in the medina, you're drinking from a system designed by 11th-century hydraulic engineers.
